root_text.an8.48:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā bhaggesu viharati susumāragire bhesakaḷāvane migadāye.
root_text.an8.48:1.2: Atha kho nakulamātā gahapatānī yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā …pe…. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho nakulamātaraṁ gahapatāniṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an8.48:2.1: “Aṭṭhahi kho, nakulamāte, dhammehi samannāgato mātugāmo kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā manāpakāyikānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an8.48:2.3: Idha, nakulamāte, mātugāmo yassa mātāpitaro bhattuno denti atthakāmā hitesino anukampakā anukampaṁ upādāya tassa hoti pubbuṭṭhāyinī pacchānipātinī kiṅkārapaṭissāvinī manāpacārinī piyavādinī.
root_text.an8.48:3.2: mātāti vā pitāti vā samaṇabrāhmaṇāti vā—
root_text.an8.48:3.3: te sakkaroti garuṁ karoti māneti pūjeti, abbhāgate ca āsanodakena paṭipūjeti.
root_text.an8.48:4.1: Ye te bhattu abbhantarā kammantā—uṇṇāti vā kappāsāti vā—tattha dakkhā hoti analasā tatrupāyāya vīmaṁsāya samannāgatā alaṁ kātuṁ alaṁ saṁvidhātuṁ.
root_text.an8.48:5.1: Yo so bhattu abbhantaro antojano—dāsāti vā pessāti vā kammakarāti vā—
root_text.an8.48:5.2: tesaṁ katañca katato jānāti akatañca akatato jānāti, gilānakānañca balābalaṁ jānāti khādanīyaṁ bhojanīyañcassa paccaṁsena saṁvibhajati.
root_text.an8.48:6.1: Yaṁ bhattā āharati dhanaṁ vā dhaññaṁ vā rajataṁ vā jātarūpaṁ vā taṁ ārakkhena guttiyā sampādeti, tattha ca hoti adhuttī athenī asoṇḍī avināsikā.
root_text.an8.48:7.1: Upāsikā kho pana hoti buddhaṁ saraṇaṁ gatā dhammaṁ saraṇaṁ gatā saṅghaṁ saraṇaṁ gatā.
root_text.an8.48:9.1: Cāgavatī kho pana hoti vigatamalamaccherena cetasā agāraṁ ajjhāvasati muttacāgā payatapāṇinī vossaggaratā yācayogā dānasaṁvibhāgaratā.
root_text.an8.48:10.1: Imehi kho, nakulamāte, aṭṭhahi dhammehi samannāgato mātugāmo kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā manāpakāyikānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjatīti.

translation_text.an8.48:0.3: With Nakula’s Mother on the Agreeable Gods
translation_text.an8.48:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Bhaggas at Crocodile’s Bellow, in the deer park at Bhesakaḷā’s Wood.
translation_text.an8.48:1.2: Then the housewife Nakula’s mother went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to her:
translation_text.an8.48:2.1: “Nakula’s mother, when they have eight qualities ladies—when their body breaks up, after death—are reborn in company with the Gods of the Agreeable Host.
translation_text.an8.48:2.3: Take the case of a lady whose mother and father give her to a husband wanting what’s best for her, out of kindness and sympathy. She would get up before him and go to bed after him, and be obliging, behaving nicely and speaking politely.
translation_text.an8.48:3.1: She honors, respects, esteems, and venerates those her husband respects, such as
translation_text.an8.48:3.2: mother and father, and ascetics and brahmins.
translation_text.an8.48:3.3: And when they arrive she serves them with a seat and water.
translation_text.an8.48:4.1: She’s deft and tireless in her husband’s household duties, such as knitting and sewing. She understands how to go about things in order to complete and organize the work.
translation_text.an8.48:5.1: She knows what work her husband’s domestic bondservants, servants, and workers have completed, and what they’ve left incomplete.
translation_text.an8.48:5.2: She knows who is sick, and who is fit or unwell. She distributes to each a fair portion of fresh and cooked foods.
translation_text.an8.48:6.1: She ensures that any income her husband earns is guarded and protected, whether money, grain, silver, or gold. She doesn’t overspend, steal, waste, or lose it.
translation_text.an8.48:7.1: She’s a lay follower who has gone for refuge to the Buddha, his teaching, and the Saṅgha.
translation_text.an8.48:8.2: She doesn’t kill living creatures, steal, commit sexual misconduct, lie, or consume beer, wine, and liquor intoxicants.
translation_text.an8.48:9.1: She’s generous. She lives at home rid of the stain of stinginess, freely generous, open-handed, loving to let go, committed to charity, loving to give and to share.
translation_text.an8.48:10.1: When they possess these eight qualities ladies—when their body breaks up, after death—are reborn in company with the Gods of the Agreeable Host.
translation_text.an8.48:11.1: She’d never look down on her husband,
translation_text.an8.48:11.2: who’s always eager to work hard,
translation_text.an8.48:12.1: And a good woman never scolds her husband
translation_text.an8.48:13.1: She gets up early, works tirelessly,
translation_text.an8.48:14.1: A lady who fulfills these duties
